summary: Russian Su-34 aircraft struck UAV command points in Dobropolye, Kucherov Yar and Nikolaevka, while Ukrainian forces reported multiple Shahed drone attacks across the south and east, including eight over Izmail and a ballistic missile over Poltava. Russia intensified KAB strikes on Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties and extensive property damage. Ukrainian forces launched coordinated attacks on occupied Crimea, igniting fires at the Tavriya power plant, a gas station and an oil‑gas depot.
key_points: Russian Su-34 jets hit UAV control sites in Dobropolye, Kucherov Yar and Nikolaevka, DNR (Source: @tass_agency/381620).
Eight Shahed drones were observed over Izmail and one over northern Rivne region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67962).
A ballistic missile was detected over Poltava city, descending toward the urban area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67931).
Russian KAB attacks on Zaporizhzhia resulted in nine strikes, four dead, six injured and damage to at least 20 houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
Ukrainian forces attacked occupied Crimea, causing fires at Tavriya thermal power plant, a gas station in the Odessa region and an oil‑gas depot (Source: @UkraineNow/82067).
A Ukrainian drone attacked a warehouse in Boryspil district, Kyiv region, igniting a fire and hospitalising a 40‑year‑old man (Source: @UkraineNow/82075).
Threats of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches were reported from Voronezh region and Taganrog, Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67929).

sections.executive_summary: Russian Su-34 strikes on UAV sites in the Donetsk People's Republic marked a significant escalation of air operations. Ukraine responded with intensified Shahed drone attacks across the south and east, while also conducting strikes on occupied Crimea that ignited multiple infrastructure fires. Concurrently, Russia intensified KAB bombardments on Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties and extensive damage.

points: Ukrainian forces launched a coordinated strike on occupied Crimea, igniting fires at the Tavriya thermal power plant, a gas station in the Odessa region and an oil‑gas depot, indicating a focus on disrupting Russian energy assets (Source: @UkraineNow/82067).

A Ukrainian drone attack on a warehouse in the Boryspil district of Kyiv region caused a fire and resulted in the hospitalization of a 40‑year‑old man with smoke inhalation (Source: @UkraineNow/82075).

Eight Shahed drones were reported over the southern port city of Izmail, with an additional Shahed observed over northern Rivne, reflecting continued UAV pressure on Russian‑held territories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67962).
Ukrainian reconnaissance noted multiple Shahed and UAV groups operating over Chernihiv, Sumy and Mykolaiv regions, though many were reported destroyed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67955).
Ukrainian drone manufacturers are seeking export opportunities in Japan, highlighting a broader strategic push for UAV capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31037).

Russian Su‑34 fighter‑bombers struck UAV command and control points in Dobropolye, Kucherov Yar and Nikolaevka within the DNR, demonstrating continued air‑ground coordination (Source: @tass_agency/381620).
Russia employed KAB precision‑guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia, delivering nine strikes that killed four civilians, wounded six and damaged at least 20 residential structures (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).

A Russian MiG‑31K interceptor, capable of carrying the hypersonic Kinzhal missile, was observed taking off from Savasleika airfield, indicating readiness for high‑speed strike missions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67949).
Russian forces reported the use of ballistic missiles over Poltava, though the missile type was not specified, highlighting a potential escalation in long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67931).

Russian equipment positioned in Belarus was claimed to be used for strikes on Zhytomyr, Rivne and Volyn regions, as well as targeting Ukrainian energy and rail infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).
Ukrainian Shahed drones were observed moving north of Kremenchuk toward the village of Hradisk, indicating pressure on the central‑eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65730).
A UAV target was reported near the settlement of Bagacheve in Cherkasy, heading toward Kirovohrad region (Source: @kpszsu/65731).

Russian Su‑34 strikes on UAV sites in Dobropolye, Kucherov Yar and Nikolaevka, all located in the eastern conflict zone, suggest a focus on degrading Ukrainian aerial reconnaissance (Source: @tass_agency/381620).
Multiple groups of attack UAVs were reported over the Sumy region, with one incident near the village of Mykhaylivsk resulting in a civilian fatality (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic missile over Poltava, a city in central‑eastern Ukraine, indicating possible deep‑strike capability usage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67931).
Shahed drones were detected over northern Rivne region, suggesting continued aerial pressure on the north‑west front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67962).
UAV activity was reported near Tomakivka in Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Zaporizhzhia, indicating cross‑regional reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/65710).
A threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch was noted from Voronezh region, which borders northern Ukraine, raising concerns of long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67929).
Ukrainian radar reported a decrease in UAV activity over Zhytomyr, though some threats persisted (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67953).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over Chernihiv region, including five groups near Slavutych and one near Semenivka (Source: @eRadarrua/97557).
Eight Shahed drones were observed over the southern port city of Izmail, highlighting intensified UAV activity in the Black Sea corridor (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67962).
Russian KAB strikes on Zaporizhzhia caused civilian casualties and extensive property damage, underscoring heightened bombardment in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).

Ukrainian forces attacked occupied Crimea, igniting fires at the Tavriya thermal power plant, a gas station and an oil‑gas depot, indicating a focus on degrading Russian logistical hubs (Source: @UkraineNow/82067).
A Russian ballistic missile threat was reported from Taganrog, Rostov region, potentially targeting southern Ukrainian territories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67948).
Ukrainian Shahed activity was also reported over the Zaporizhzhia region, with two drones observed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67925).
Russian Su‑34 aircraft conducted strikes on UAV command sites in the Donetsk People's Republic, demonstrating continued air superiority missions (Source: @tass_agency/381620).
Ukrainian air alerts were issued over Kyiv multiple times, reflecting ongoing missile and UAV threats (Source: @eRadarrua/97548, @eRadarrua/97567).
A Russian MiG‑31K, capable of launching the hypersonic Kinzhal, was observed taking off, indicating potential high‑speed strike capability deployment (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67949).
Ballistic missile activity over Poltava was reported, suggesting use of longer‑range air‑launched systems (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67931).
Ukrainian forces employed Shahed drones extensively across multiple fronts, with reports of both successful interceptions and ongoing threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67955).

key_developments: Russian Su‑34 jets struck UAV command points in Dobropolye, Kucherov Yar and Nikolaevka, DNR, degrading Ukrainian aerial reconnaissance (Source: @tass_agency/381620).
Eight Shahed drones over Izmail and one over northern Rivne indicate intensified UAV pressure on Russian‑held southern and north‑west fronts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67962).
KAB precision‑guided bomb attacks on Zaporizhzhia caused four civilian deaths, six injuries and damage to at least 20 homes, marking a severe escalation in bombardment (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
Ukrainian strikes on occupied Crimea ignited fires at Tavriya power plant, a gas station and an oil‑gas depot, targeting Russian energy infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82067).
A ballistic missile was detected over Poltava, descending toward the city, suggesting use of long‑range strike assets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67931).
Threats of Iskander‑M missile launches from Voronezh and Taganrog regions were reported, raising the risk of strategic ballistic attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67929, @mon1tor_ua/67948).
A Russian MiG‑31K interceptor, capable of carrying the Kinzhal hypersonic missile, was observed taking off, indicating readiness for high‑speed strike missions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67949).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air assets continue to target Ukrainian UAV command nodes, while the deployment of hypersonic-capable platforms like the MiG‑31K suggests a potential escalation to high‑speed strikes. Ukraine's increased use of Shahed drones across multiple fronts, combined with ballistic missile threats from Russian territory, creates a layered aerial threat environment. The KAB bombardments on Zaporizhzhia and the reported use of Belarusian equipment to strike western regions indicate Russia's intent to pressure both civilian and military targets, raising the risk of broader civilian casualties and infrastructure disruption.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian regional commands have intensified air defense alerts in Kyiv and reported successful interceptions of several UAVs, yet civilian infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia and Crimea remains vulnerable. Russian forces appear to be concentrating airstrike capabilities in the east and south, while also leveraging Belarusian relay stations to extend reach into western oblasts. Both sides are likely to reinforce air defense and artillery assets in the most contested zones, with potential redeployment of mobile SAM systems to protect critical infrastructure.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of Shahed concentrations near strategic ports and energy facilities, suggesting a Ukrainian focus on disrupting supply lines. Russian Su‑34 sortie logs and MiG‑31K activity should be tracked for indications of upcoming hypersonic missile launches. The reported Iskander‑M threats from Voronezh and Taganrog warrant heightened radar surveillance and pre‑emptive counter‑rocket measures. Continued assessment of Belarusian relay equipment usage is essential to gauge the expanding strike envelope against western Ukrainian regions.
